data.description:
Replace ~15 inline-style hero overrides (

) with the .text-display utility. Sites: EventsPage, PeoplePage, StoryPage, LabPage, PoliticalClubPage, NeighborhoodPage, LiberalArts, BadgePlayground, Home, Navbar (hero contexts only — non-hero Jacquard uses stay inline). Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md. Why: Inline-style trio (fontWeight: 300, textTransform: 'uppercase', fontStyle: 'normal') is duplicated across ~15 sites — a maintenance trap and a missed reuse opportunity. .text-display encodes exactly this pattern. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation. Acceptance: Zero inline 'fontWeight: 300, textTransform: uppercase, fontStyle: normal' triples remain in src/. All hero text renders identically to pre-migration. Visual diff is null.
data.title: Hero display migration — adopt .text-display utility

data.body:
Review verdict: PASS

Diff stats: 9 files, 11 insertions, 11 deletions. Pure class-string swaps; no other lines touched.

Acceptance criteria:
- [x] All 11 sites migrated per plan (Events x3, People, Story, Lab, Home h2, PoliticalClub, Neighborhood, Campus, LiberalArts)
- [x] HouseBannerGrid kept on display-roman (with explicit letter-spacing 0.15em — intentional escape hatch)
- [x] rg display-roman src/ returns 1 call-site (HouseBannerGrid:22) + 2 definitional/comment lines in index.css
- [x] Build clean (3.59s); typecheck clean; tests 143 pass / 4 fail (matches baseline, no regression); DESIGN.md lint 0 errors
- [x] No regressions; preserved modifiers (text-white, text-center, mb-3/4/6/10/12, md:mb-6/10) intact at every site
- [x] PRD alignment: bold-hero aesthetic in FRAC-22 reinforced by larger .text-display sizing; no contradictions

Visual-risk judgment (cold read of code):
- LabPage:27 long copy (A Research Institute Doing Gain of Function Research on the Golden Age Virus — 87 chars): At md:text-7xl (72px) with leading 1.1 and uppercase tracking 0.04em, this will wrap to ~3-4 lines desktop. Denser than before due to leading 1.3 -> 1.1. Plausible visual risk but not broken — worth eyeballing in browser but acceptable as a content-vs-system tradeoff. Not blocking.
- NeighborhoodPage:23 mobile jump: text-xl (20px) -> text-5xl (48px) for a 5-word phrase. Big jump but intentional unification of hero scale across site; matches sibling pages now. mb-3 mobile collar preserved. Not a bug.
- LiberalArts:13 dropped sm: breakpoint: old text-2xl/sm:text-4xl/md:text-6xl -> new text-5xl/md:text-7xl (2-tier). The sm: intermediate vanishes, but no other site uses an sm: hero tier, so consistency wins. 5-word phrase (Tech, Entrepreneurship, Rhetoric, Civics) will wrap to 2-3 lines mobile but readable.

Findings:
- Mechanically faithful migration. No extraneous edits. No accidental modifier drops. No leftover font-serif/text-4xl/text-6xl/leading-[1.3] on any migrated hero.
- Home.tsx h2 migration: previous explicit text-5xl md:text-7xl exactly matches .text-display sizes — this site sees no size change, only leading 1.3 -> 1.1 (slightly tighter A Golden / Age Protocol 2-line composition). Clean.
- Home.tsx:35 mono paragraph (untouched), Navbar Jacquard wordmark (untouched), BadgePlayground (untouched), HouseBannerGrid (untouched) — all confirmed.
- rg inline-style trio check: only non-hero contexts remain (Home mono :35, Navbar :80/:102/:296). Trio pattern fully retired from heroes.
- All three risk-flagged sites have judgment calls (size jumps, dropped sm:, long copy) that are intentional consequences of system unification, not implementation defects. They are visual-design decisions worth eyeballing live but not migration bugs.
- One Lattice bookkeeping note (not blocking): the feature branch checkout does not contain this task in its .lattice/ state (the branch is behind on Lattice events from master). The branch-local .lattice/plans/FRAC-52.md is stale (describes an older Campus bootcamp rename use of the FRAC-52 short-code). The current hero-migration task lives in master worktree state. Worth a quick reconciliation but does not affect the code change.

Recommendation: merge.
